conv2d.name: Conv2D patch projection
notes: input layout: PyTorch NCHW -> TF NHWC -> compare as NCHW

layernorm.name: LayerNormProxy channel LayerNorm
notes: LayerNormProxy evidence: NCHW -> NHWC, channel-only LayerNorm axis=-1, NHWC -> NCHW

gelu.name: GELU
notes: No checkpoint tensor is used for GELU because it is parameter-free.
PyTorch DAT uses nn.GELU() default semantics, so TensorFlow modes are compared against PyTorch default GELU.

mlp.name: Isolated TransformerMLP linear1-GELU-linear2
notes: tested isolated Linear -> GELU -> Linear path only; dropout is inactive and no spatial rearrange is included
PyTorch GELU mode: default; TensorFlow GELU mode: exact

warnings: stage_b_basic_layer_parity.py:48: You are using `torch.load` with `weights_only=False` (the current default value), which uses the default pickle module implicitly. It is possible to construct malicious pickle data which will execute arbitrary code during unpickling (See https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/main/SECURITY.md#untrusted-models for more details). In a future release, the default value for `weights_only` will be flipped to `True`. This limits the functions that could be executed during unpickling. Arbitrary objects will no longer be allowed to be loaded via this mode unless they are explicitly allowlisted by the user via `torch.serialization.add_safe_globals`. We recommend you start setting `weights_only=True` for any use case where you don't have full control of the loaded file. Please open an issue on GitHub for any issues related to this experimental feature.
